results.title: Archer Daniels Midland AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Archer Daniels Midland (ADM), a global leader in agricultural processing and food ingredients, has positioned itself as a pioneer in applying artificial intelligence across its operations. Under the leadership of CIO Kristy Folkwein, ADM has embarked on a comprehensive digital transformation that leverages AI for everything from flavor innovation to supply chain optimization[1]. The company has developed 'ChatADM,' an internal secure generative AI platform that has enabled prototyping of over 35 applications[2]. ADM's AI initiatives focus on capturing tribal knowledge, improving operational efficiency, and driving innovation in food science, with particular success in using generative AI to accelerate flavor development and reduce costs[1]. The company is also leveraging AI partnerships, such as its collaboration with Brightseed to decode gut microbiome interactions using AI-powered bioactive discovery[3].
...

### ChatADM - Internal Generative AI Platform
...
Secure internal generative AI platform enabling AI experimentation across the organization
...
Has enabled prototyping of over 35 applications, from automating routine tasks to interpreting video for equipment monitoring
...
### AI-Powered Flavor Innovation
...
Using generative AI to accelerate food science innovation and flavor development
...
AI models suggest flavor combinations that human experts hadn't considered, leading to cost-effective, high-quality flavors. Teams collaborate with flavorists to develop new flavors faster and at lower costs
...
### Machine Learning Customer Analytics
...
ML models for customer targeting and business intelligence
...
Used machine learning models to identify new customers, prioritize cross-selling opportunities, and predict potential customer attrition with extraordinary results
...
ADM uses generative AI to accelerate flavor innovation by analyzing recipes and ingredient data across the organization. AI models suggest flavor combinations that human experts hadn't considered, helping teams develop new flavors faster and at lower costs.
...
ChatADM is ADM's internal, secure generative AI platform that has enabled prototyping of over 35 applications. The company emphasizes protecting proprietary data while fostering AI experimentation across the organization.
...
ADM applies AI for supply chain optimization, predictive analytics, and operational efficiency. This includes using machine learning for customer targeting, demand forecasting, and automating routine tasks across their global network.
...
ADM partners with Brightseed to use AI for microbiome research, specifically using Brightseed's Forager AI platform to discover bioactives in plants and understand molecular interactions between dietary plants and gut microbes.
...

production • data mining • credits • substrate • fermentation • basic • profitability • fermentations • troubleshooting • compliance • downstream • process • project work • manufacturing • packaging • financial analysis • tax • data scientist • enzyme • project management • scale • director • facilities • facility management • process research • plant operations • tax credits • interim management • technical skills • fermentation technology • processing • mentoring • production facilities • fda • across • teams • recycling management • leadership • fish • managing • driving operational excellence • food packaging • financial management • project manager • fda compliance • biomass conversion • mining projects • industrial process • technology • wastewater • operational excellence • permit • senior project manager • advisory • npdes • data mining techniques • mining • manufacturing analytics • management • conversion • analytics • per • operations management • data • research • ethanol
...
Andrew Ng 's conversation with Bain & Company hit on something I see every day with clients.  The agentic era is not just about better #AI tools. It is about redesigning how work gets done.
...
Last week I spoke at a DataCamp webinar on Claude in the Enterprise. The conversation reinforced something I keep seeing across mining, aviation, and industrial operations: AI stalls are a transformation problem, not a technology problem.
...
Technology is rarely the obstacle. In practice, the hardest work in enterprise AI adoption is changing how people actually operate- redesigning processes, building new habits, and getting organizations to trust outputs they didn't produce themselves.
...
Start with the problem, not the technology. Organizations that define a specific business outcome first, then identify where AI helps, get traction. Those that chase the technology and look for problems produce expensive pilots with thin ROI.
...
Governance before scale. Strategic guardrails- what to experiment on, and why, need to exist before the first wave of enthusiasm, not after the first incident.
...
On the workforce question: the
...
is real. The opportunity I
...
is in redirecting human judgment toward work that matters more, but that transition requires deliberate investment in people, not just technology.

results.title: Ashwami Dalvi
highlights:
A problem-solver with 5+ years of Data Engineering experience building scalable pipelines and analytics platforms across finance and agribusiness. At ADM, I own end-to-end data engineering for the Pricing Analytics, InsightOps, and Product-to-Consumer (P2C) teams — cutting pipeline latency by 40% and powering dashboards used by 100+ business users. Currently leading platform modernization initiatives on the Databricks ecosystem — leveraging Genie, Databricks, and Unity Catalog to modernize our data platform and unlock AI-ready infrastructure at enterprise scale. Deeply curious about the intersection of data engineering and AI — exploring how LLMs, ML pipelines, and intelligent automation can be woven into modern data platforms. I vibe-code side projects to stay sharp on the cutting edge. Core stack: Azure Data Factory · Synapse Analytics · Databricks (Unity Catalog · AI Genie. AI/BI dashboards ) · Delta Lake · PySpark · dbt · SAP Datasphere · Denodo · Power BI · Qlik Compose · Ab Initio Previously at LTI (Larsen & Toubro Infotech), built Spark-based pipelines for Citi Bank's equity compensation and credit risk systems and performing ETL using Ab Initio
...

Designed data mart solutions and Power BI dashboards that brought pricing intelligence to non-technical stakeholders in the Animal Nutrition business unit. InsightOps: Engineered KPI workflows and data pipelines in Databricks (Unity Catalog) to deliver business intelligence across operational teams, directly informing executive decision-making. Translated complex business requirements into production-grade code, with dashboards consumed by 100+ business users across ADM. Platform Modernization & P2C (Current focus): Leading platform modernization to the Databricks ecosystem (DBRX, Genie, Unity Catalog), enabling AI-ready infrastructure at enterprise scale. Building NPD (New Product Development) data solutions on SAP Datasphere for the Product-to-Consumer team, supporting end-to-end product lifecycle visibility.

...
I lead digital and analytics strategy and delivery supporting our global businesses at ADM. I am passionate about the transformative impact evolving technology can have on our company, our employees, our business and grower/producer partners, our consumers, and our communities. I started my career at the intersection of data and advanced analytics + strategic thinking + digital technology enablement and in my 13+ years of consulting helped many leading companies in nearly every industry build these capabilities at scale managing the balance between digital (fast, new) and foundational (global, deep) technologies. At ADM, I am excited to forge all these disciplines (digital, analytics, data, business strategy, great talent) together and do so within our IT organization to help create an integrated but flexible global technology model with capabilities that are reinforcing vs. isolated. Leveraging our rapidly evolving technology foundation, my team and I have great aspirations for how far forward we can propel the global agribusiness and nutrition industries.

...
Archer Daniels Midland Companyabout 12 hours ago
...
Columbus, OH, United States
...
## Job Description
...
## Job Description
...
Regional Engineering Manager - Columbus, NE
...
This is a permanent, full-time, exempt-level position reporting to the Manager of Engineering Services.
...
## Position Summary
...
The Regional Engineering Manager will serve as the primary technical authority and "Owner's Engineer" for assigned operational and capital projects within the region.
...
This role provides leadership and oversight across all engineering disciplines to ensure safe, reliable, and efficient technical project delivery for ADM's processing plants.
...
The position acts as the central point of contact for all engineering matters, interfacing closely with Mechanical Integrity, Structural Integrity, Engineering Services, Reliability, Safety, Automation, Plant Operations, and CAPEX teams to ensure alignment with corporate standards and operational objectives.
...
## Key Responsibilities
...
- Function as the Owner's Engineer, providing technical leadership, assurance, and oversight for all engineering activities on regional projects
...
central engineering interface
...
project teams, EPC contractors
...
and internal stakeholders including Mechanical Integrity, Structural Integrity, Engineering Services, Reliability, Safety, Plant Operations, and CAPEX groups
...
- Lead the preparation, coordination, and review of detailed engineering design packages across multiple disciplines (process, mechanical, electrical, civil/structural, instrumentation, automation, etc.)
...
- Develop and assemble technical bid packages, defining scope of work, design basis, specifications, and evaluation criteria
...
- Review and evaluate EPC proposals, engineering deliverables, and contractor submittals to ensure compliance with project requirements, standards, and best practices
...
- Manage full engineering scope, schedule, budget, and resource allocation for small capital/OPEX projects
...
- Provide technical assurance throughout project development, design, and execution phases
...
- Collaborate with project management, procurement, construction, and operations teams to ensure seamless project execution and alignment with business objectives
...
- Serve as local site engineering liaison and support business development and capital planning by providing technical input and feasibility assessments
...
- Support on-going continuous improvement and synergy development across ADM's business functional units
...
- Mentor and develop junior engineering staff
...
## Key Competencies
...
## Required Qualifications
...
- Bachelor's degree in Engineering: (Chemical, Mechanical, Industrial, Electrical, Civil, or related discipline)
- Professional licenses: such as PE/PMP or equivalent
...
- 10+ years of progressive engineering experience: in the process industries - such as oil & gas, specialty chemicals, food & beverage, or agricultural processing
...
- Proven track record: in detailed engineering design, and small and large capital project engineering
...
- Demonstrated ability: to act as Owner's Engineer, coordinating multi-discipline engineering efforts and interfacing across diverse functional teams
...
- Experience: preparing and evaluating technical bid packages and EPC proposals
...
- Strong knowledge: of engineering standards, codes, and regulatory requirements applicable to process industry projects
...
- Excellent communication: leadership, and stakeholder management skills
...
- Proficiency: with engineering design tools, project management systems, and digital collaboration platforms
...

results.title: Account Manager - Remote | Hirevector
highlights: position. Position Summary: The Nutrition Division of Archer Daniels Midland Co. is actively seeking an initiative-taking and experienced Account Manager to join our dynamic sales team. The ideal candidate will have a proven record in sales, exceptional leadership skills, and a deep understanding of customer relationship management. Individual will be responsible for selling dairy flavors, processed cheese and custom cheese sauce to food manufacturers. Additional responsibilities include identifying opportunities for food flavorings, specialty ingredients, and complete formula solutions from the ADM Nutrition portfolio to customers. This role is responsible for driving sales growth, opening new markets, managing key accounts, processing pricing requests and developing long-term relationships. Job Responsibilities: Account Management: Develop and maintain strong relationships with key accounts. Serve as the primary point of contact for clients, addressing their needs and concerns promptly. Monitor customer satisfaction and work to resolve any issues that arise. Sales Strategy and Execution: Develop and implement effective sales strategies to achieve sales targets and objectives. Identify new business opportunities and potential clients. Prepare and deliver compelling sales presentations and proposals. Revenue Growth: Drive revenue growth by identifying upselling and cross-selling opportunities within existing accounts. Negotiate marketing programs and close sales deals to meet or exceed sales quotas Product Knowledge: Serve as the primary point of contact for product knowledge, manufacturing process, and application of product line. Provide insights and recommendations to improve sales strategies and product offerings Team Collaboration: Collaborate with internal teams, including marketing, product development, and customer support, to ensure seamless customer experience Reporting and Documentation: Maintain accurate and up-to-date records of all sales activities, customer interactions, and account information Prepare regular reports on sales performance, account status, and market trends for senior management Demonstrate strong organization, time management and communication skills Required Skills: Strong negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills Ability to build and maintain long-term relationships with clients Proficiency in CRM software and Microsoft Office Suite. Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities Ability to work independently and as part of a team Willingness to travel up to 50% as needed Education Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Business, Marketing, or Culinary Arts Required Experience: Minimum of 3 years of experience in customer-facing activity with focus on account and business opportunity management Experience in flavors, processed cheese or ingredient sales Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets Desired Experience: Culinary arts experience as a Chef or food science and development position Advanced degree or relevant certifications. Familiarity with Salesforce, Microsoft Office, SAP Excited about this role but don’t think you meet every requirement listed? We encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this role or another one of our openings. ADM requires the successful completion of a background check. REF:107619BR City New Berlin State WI - Wisconsin Ref ID #LI-JW1 About ADM At ADM, we unlock the power of nature to provide access to nutrition worldwide. With industry-advancing innovations, a complete portfolio of ingredients and solutions to meet any taste, and a commitment to sustainability, we give customers an edge in solving the nutritional challenges of today and tomorrow. We’re a global leader in human and animal nutrition and the world’s premier agricultural origination and processing company. Our breadth, depth, insights, facilities and logistical expertise give us unparalleled capabilities to meet needs for food, beverages,

Archer is an aerospace company based in San Jose, California building an all-electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft with a mission to advance the benefits of sustainable air mobility. We are designing, manufacturing, and operating an all-electric aircraft that can carry four passengers while producing minimal noise.
...
What You’ll Do
...
- Design, implement, and evaluate novel machine learning and deep learning algorithms, concentrating on Large Language Models (LLMs), automated speech recognition (transcription), and unified multimodal architectures.
...
- Iterate on AI model development, starting from the data needed for training, architecture, input/output representations, evaluation, and deployment for complex sequence-to-sequence and multimodal tasks.
...
- Collaborate with other research engineers to prototype and validate complex solutions from academic literature in natural language processing and speech.
...
- Conduct experiments to benchmark new techniques and evaluate model behavior.
...
- Develop tools and frameworks to support scalable and reproducible research and development.
...
- Communicate research findings to leadership in a concise, and convincing manner.
...

...
- Assist in transitioning research prototypes into production-ready systems.
...
Minimum Education Requirement: Master’s degree in Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, Computational Sciences, Machine Learning, Informatics.
...
Minimum Experience Requirements: AI/ML Engineer, Software Engineer, Research Scientist, AI Research Engineer or related job title in which 3 years of experience with developing and supporting production machine learning systems, including model deployment, monitoring, versioning, and retraining, using modern observability and experimentation tools (e.g., Grafana, Prometheus, Statsig); building ML and deep learning models using Python and PyTorch, including NLP tools (e.g., Huggingface, NLTK, spaCy, transformer-based models); with largescale data pipelines using distributed processing frameworks (e.g., Spark, Databricks); and collaborating with cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into ML solutions PLUS 1 year of experience developing computer vision or multimodal ML systems (e.g., OpenCV, YOLO, vision transformers) were gained.
...
Alternate Education Requirement: PhD in Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, Computational Sciences, Machine Learning, Informatics.
...

...

results.title: Advisor - Data Architect, Data Foundry job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3059969
highlights:
# Advisor - Data Architect, Data Foundry
...
Employer Eli Lilly and Company Location San Francisco, California Start date Jul 5, 2026
...
Data Foundry is a multidisciplinary team within DTP that enables AI-native drug discovery through four integrated pillars: Architecture4Insight (data infrastructure and scientific software), Methods4Insight (analytical and computational methods), Automation & Scale4Insight (lab automation and agentic workflows), and Preparedness4Insight (data governance and readiness). These pillars empower every Lilly scientist to make optimal decisions by providing seamless access to data, insights, and AI-driven capabilities—serving both human scientists and autonomous AI agents.
...
We are seeking Data Architects at multiple levels to design and build the data infrastructure that makes AI-native drug discovery possible. You will create the schemas, ontologies, data models, knowledge graphs, and platform architectures that transform raw scientific data into machine-actionable, FAIR-compliant, insight-ready assets—serving both discovery scientists and autonomous AI agents.
...
This role is the foundation of Architecture4Insight. Everything the software engineering team builds—pipelines, APIs, prototypes—depends on the data models and platform architecture this team designs. You will work with deep knowledge of scientific data (chemical, biological, HTE, automation-generated) to create custom-fit solutions, then partner with Tech@Lilly to scale and maintain them. The role spans three focus areas depending on expertise: data modeling & ontologies, data platform & lakehouse architecture, and knowledge graph & specialized data systems. You will independently design schemas, select technologies, and make build-vs-buy recommendations for their domain.
...
- Design and implement data lakehouse architecture using modern platforms (Databricks, Snowflake, or equivalent), including data storage patterns, partitioning strategies, and query optimization.
...
- Build and optimize ETL/ELT pipelines using Spark, dbt, or similar tools to transform raw scientific data into analytical and ML-ready formats.
...
- Implement real-time and streaming data integration (Kafka, Kinesis, event-driven patterns) connecting LIMS, instruments, and lab automation systems to the data infrastructure.
...
- Design and implement knowledge graphs (Neo4j, Amazon Neptune, TigerGraph) that capture molecular, target, pathway, and experimental relationships across the discovery landscape.
...
- Architect specialized data solutions: array databases (TileDB) for genomics/imaging, document stores (MongoDB) for experimental records, and vector databases for embedding-based retrieval supporting ML and RAG workflows.
...
- Build query and traversal patterns that enable scientists and AI agents to ask relational questions across the entire data landscape.
...
Methods4Insight to design
...
that support analytical model training, deployment, and evaluation.
...
- M.S. or PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, Information Science, or related STEM field
...
- MS (with 6+ years ) and PhD (with 2+ years) of data architecture, data engineering, or scientific informatics experience.
...
- Deep expertise in at least one of the focus areas: relational databases, data modeling and ontology engineering, data platform and lakehouse architecture (Databricks, Snowflake, Spark), or knowledge graph and specialized database systems (Neo4j, Neptune, MongoDB, TileDB)
...
- Data lakehouse architecture experience: Databricks (Delta Lake, Unity Catalog), Snowflake, or equivalent; ETL/ELT with Spark, dbt.
...
) and event-driven
...
- Experience with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector) and embedding-based retrieval for ML/RAG applications.
...
- Experience with C, C++, or Rust for performance-critical data processing; familiarity with HPC data I/O patterns for large-scale scientific computations.
